headline: What to give to Richie Rich
summary: A replica fighter-bomber, a Le Mans legend and more things for people with bottomless pockets
body_text:
No fewer than 11 Ferrari 250 Testa Rossas started the 24 Hours of Le Mans in 1958. One of them went on to win, but the most visually striking – in Bianco Cervino with blue stripes – was privately entered and came in a creditable seventh. In collaboration with Ferrari, Hedley Studios has created a 75 per cent scale electric replica of that car in its post-race state, complete with handpainted, race-weathered effects by Hedley’s in-house artist. (This commitment to authenticity extends to places you can’t see, including under the bonnet.) Fully drivable on private roads or tracks, it has a range of up to 90km and can be recharged via a repurposed, original-style fuel cap.

The P-51 Mustang fighter-bomber is generally associated with US Air Force action during the second world war and Korean war, although it was eventually deployed by some 25 countries. An original could set you back around $4mn, on top of which you’d have the headache of keeping it airworthy and sourcing spare parts. Ger
